program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri ve ilgili Mühendislik disiplinine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimi
Bu alanlardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin çözümünde kullanabilme becerisi
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi
Bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisi
Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisi
Bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama becerisi
Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları, seçme ve kullanma becerisi
Bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisi
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya disipline özgü araştırma konularının incelenmesi için deney tasarlama
Deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisi
Disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi
Bireysel çalışma becerisi
Sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi
En az bir yabancı dil bilgisi
Etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, etkin sunum yapabilme
Tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme
Açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği konusunda farkındalık
Bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisi
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk
Mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi
Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgi
Girişimcilik, yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık
Sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi
Mühendislik uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgi
Mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Engineering and Career Planning
courses.purpose: This course introduces students who are beginning their Computer Engineering education to the working areas of engineering, and especially computer engineering. It also aims to develop their ability to think about the history, philosophy, and fundamentals of hardware and software, while increasing awareness through career-related information and activities.
courses.description: This course covers the history and working areas of the computer engineering profession, basic hardware and software concepts, algorithmic thinking, introductory programming, career planning, and professional written communication skills.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes the working areas of engineering and especially computer engineering.
Gains the ability to understand and evaluate the fundamentals of computer hardware and software.
Understands the principles of programming in languages such as C and Assembly and can write simple programs.
Can evaluate the differences between high-level and low-level languages and the suitability of a language to a problem.
Can write a resume and a cover letter.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Computer Engineering, Binary Numbers, Number Systems, Introduction to Computer Architecture.
Hafta 2: Basic Assembly Programming Language, Introduction to C Programming and Control Structures.
Hafta 3: Assembly Programming Language, Simpsim, Introduction to Variables in C Programming.
Hafta 4: Data Structures, Data Structures and Arrays in C Programming, Introduction to Arduino Programming.
Hafta 5: Computer Architecture, C Programming, Loops.
Hafta 6: Formatted Input/Output, Introduction to Computer Networks, C Functions.
Hafta 8: Software Engineering concept and software types. Introduction to Programming Languages. File input/output operations in C.
Hafta 9: Algorithms and flowcharts, Design of Logic Circuits, Algorithms and flowcharts. Database Systems. C Pointer Structures.

Hafta 10: What is a career? Explanation of the concepts of skill, ability, competence, career and career management, and increasing students' awareness. National and International Exchange Programs: Informing students about exchange and scholarship programs that support undergraduate education.

Hafta 11: Sector Days - Private Sector. A panel with managers from different fields such as service, finance, and manufacturing to provide information about employers' expectations from new graduates and career opportunities in these sectors. It is recommended that inspiring people who graduated from the university hosting the panel and achieved success in the private sector also participate.

Hafta 12: Sector Days - Academia. A panel with academics to raise awareness among students considering an academic career in different disciplines and to explain the requirements of academic life today. It is recommended that inspiring people who graduated from the university hosting the panel and achieved success in academia also participate.
Hafta 13: Preparing a Resume and Cover Letter. Training on preparing a resume and cover letter.
resources: https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL2671213A/Glenn_Brookshear

courses.name: Science and Technology
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the historical development of science and technology and how processes of knowledge production and scientific thinking have been shaped in different periods. It also aims to enable students to critically evaluate the social, economic, and cultural effects of scientific and technological transformations and interpret the science-society relationship analytically.
courses.description: The course covers the basic concepts of science and technology, the processes of knowledge production and accumulation, and the historical background of scientific developments. It also examines the development of scientific thought and technological transformations from Antiquity to the present, industrial revolutions, and the relationship between science and technology in modern society.
learning_outcomes: The student can compare the fundamental transformations in science and technology across historical periods from Antiquity to the Fourth Industrial Revolution and interpret their effects on social structures.

The student can analyze the relationship between scientific developments and economic, cultural, and industrial changes; especially evaluate the technology-society interaction in the context of industrial revolutions.

The student can critically evaluate the place of science in modern society, the concept of technoscience, and the science-society relationship through case studies or current developments, and express opinions in an academic manner orally or in writing.

The student can explain the basic concepts of science and technology and the processes of knowledge production and accumulation; and discuss the role of discovery, classification, storage, and dissemination in scientific development.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and the Definition of Science and Technology
Hafta 2: What is Knowledge? The Formation, Development, and Storage of Knowledge. Discovery, Collection, Classification.
Hafta 4: Science in the Middle Ages
Hafta 6: The Age of Enlightenment and Scientific Developments
Hafta 10: The Third Industrial Period and the Transition to the Fourth Period Today
Hafta 12: The Relationship Between Science and Society, the Place of Science in Modern Society, Technoscience.
Hafta 13: The Age of Artificial Intelligence
resources: https://link.springer.com/series/10085
TEAMS
<www.mehmetgokturk.com>

courses.name: Principles of Atatürk and the History of Turkish Revolution I
courses.purpose: To provide information about modern history, Mustafa Kemal Atatürk's reforms and principles, and the achievements of the Republic of Turkey.
courses.description: This course examines the political, social, and intellectual transformations in the late Ottoman Empire, beginning with the modernization process in the 19th century and continuing through the Tanzimat and Constitutional periods and the Committee of Union and Progress era. It analyzes the causes and consequences of the Tripoli and Balkan Wars and World War I, the occupations after the Armistice of Mudros, the Paris Peace Conference, and the political developments of the period. The course also covers the preparatory phase of the National Struggle, Mustafa Kemal Pasha's arrival in Anatolia, the congresses period, the Amasya Circular, the activities of the Representative Committee, the acceptance of the National Pact, the occupation of Istanbul, the opening of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, the structure of the TBMM, and the role of the press during the National Struggle.
learning_outcomes: Students will gain knowledge about the modernization efforts of the Ottoman Empire.
Students will understand the philosophy of Turkish modernization.
Students will gain knowledge about the preparatory and armed struggle phases of the National Struggle period.
Students will gain knowledge about the organization of the National Struggle.
Students will be able to compare the collapse of the Ottoman Empire with World War I.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course. General information about the course and concepts
Hafta 2: General structure of the Ottoman Empire. 19th century Ottoman modernization (Tulip Era, Selim III Period 1789-1807, Mahmud II Period 1808-1839)

Hafta 3: Tanzimat Period; First Constitutional Period, from the Ottoman Society of Union to the Committee of Union and Progress (Ottoman Society of Union and Young Turks - Declaration of the Second Constitutional Period); Second Constitutional Period; 31 March Incident
Hafta 4: Reforms and political life (intellectual currents of the Second Constitutional Period); Tripoli War; Balkan Wars
Hafta 5: World War I and the Ottoman Empire; causes of World War I and the outbreak of the war; World War I from the Ottoman perspective; consequences of World War I
Hafta 6: Armistice of Mudros, 30 October 1918 (signing of the armistice and its main provisions); implementation of the Armistice of Mudros and occupations
Hafta 7: Political developments during the Armistice period and the Paris Peace Conference, 18 January 1919 (general overview of the conference and decisions regarding Turkish lands)
Hafta 8: Midterm exam. Evaluation of the Paris Peace Conference.
Hafta 9: Political events after the armistice; Istanbul governments from the armistice to the occupation of Izmir; occupation of Izmir and associations in the National Struggle
Hafta 10: Mustafa Kemal Pasha during the Armistice period (his passage to Anatolia, appointment as Inspector of the 9th Army, landing in Samsun on 19 May 1919)

Hafta 11: Period of circulars and congresses (Havza Circular, Amasya Circular, Erzurum Congress, Sivas Congress, other congresses); Representative Committee and Istanbul governments; Amasya Meeting; Mustafa Kemal Pasha and the Representative Committee's arrival in Ankara
Hafta 12: Acceptance of the National Pact, occupation of Istanbul, and the opening of the First Grand National Assembly on 23 April 1920
Hafta 13: General characteristics of the TBMM, laws enacted
Hafta 14: The press in the National Struggle
resources: GÖKBİLGİN, M. Tayyib, Milli Mücadele Başlarken Mondros Mütarekesinden Sivas Kongresine, (2C), Türkiye İş Bankası Kültür Yayınları, Ankara, 1959.
GÜNEŞ, İhsan, Birinci Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi’nin Düşünsel Yapısı, Eskişehir, 1985.
KIRÇAK, Çağlar, Meşrutiyetten Günümüze Gericilik, (2 C) Basım, İmge Kitabevi, Ankara, 1994.
MERAY, Seha L – OLCAY, Osman, Osmanlı İmparatorluğunun Çöküş Belgeleri (Mondros Bırakışması, Sevr Antlaşması, İlgili Belgeler), Ankara Üniversitesi Siyasal Bilgiler Fakültesi Yayınları, Ankara, 1977.
OLCAY, Osman, Sevres Andlaşmasına Doğru (Çeşitli Konferans ve Toplantıların Tutanakları ve Bunlara İlişkin Belgeler), Ankara Üniversitesi Siyasal Bilgiler Fakültesi Yayınları, Ankara, 1981.
ORBAY, Rauf, Cehennem Değirmeni Siyasi Hatıralarım, I, Emre Yayınları, İstanbul, 1993.
ÖZTOPRAK, İzzet, Türk ve Batı Kamuoyunda Milli Mücadele, Atatürk Kültür, Dil ve Tarih Yüksek Kurumu, Türk Tarih Kurumu Yayınları, Ankara, 1989.
RENOUVIN, Pıerre, Birinci Dünya Savaşı 1914-1918, 3. Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi, Ankuzem Yayınları, Ankara, 2009.
Başlangıcından Günümüze Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Tarihi, (Editör Prof. Dr. Temuçin Faik Ertan), Siyasal Kitabevi, Ankara, 2011.

courses.name: Physics I
courses.purpose: Veritabanı sistemlerinin temelleri üzerinde çalışma ve bu temelden yola çıkılarak veri tabanı tasarımının yapılması gibi konularda yetkinliklerin öğrencilere kazandırılması

courses.name: Turkish I
courses.purpose: Dilin duygu, düşünce ve kültür ilişkisini açıklayarak öğrencilere dil bilinci kazandırmak, Türkçenin tarihi süreçleri ve gramatikal yapısına ilişkin bilgileri güçlendirerek dil ve anlatımda görülen eksiklikleri gidermek ve öğrencilerin Türk dili ile ilgili duyarlılıklarını artırmak.
courses.description: Dilin tanımı, özellikleri; düşünce, kültür ve toplumla ilişkisi, dillerin kökeni ve dünya dilleri, Türk dilinin genetik ve yapısal özellikleri, Türk dilinin tarihi dönemleri ile ses, yapı ve söz dizimi ile ilgili konular dersin içeriğini oluşturmaktadır.
learning_outcomes: Türkçenin temel özelliklerini tanımlamak, dil ile kültür ve dil ile duygu/biliş arasındaki ilişkiyi yorumlayabilmek.
Türkçenin tarihini bilmek ve dünya dilleriyle karşılaştırabilmek
Ses, yapı ve cümle bilgisine dayalı olarak Türkçeyi doğru ve etkili kullanabilmek.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Dil nedir? Dilin Özellikleri Nelerdir? Dillerin Doğuşu Dil-Duygu- Düşünce Bağlantısı
Hafta 2: Kültür Nedir? Dil - Kültür İlişkisi Dilin Kültür ve Millet Varlığındaki Yeri
Hafta 3: Yerüzündeki Diller Türkçenin Dünya Dilleri arasındaki Yeri Yapı ve Köken Bakımından Diller
Hafta 4: Türk Dilinin Gelişmesi ve Tarihi Devirleri (Türkçenin Yaşı, Lehçe ve Ağızlar, Türklerin Tarih Boyunca Kullandığı Alfabeler)
Hafta 5: Türk Dilinin Gelişmesi ve Tarihi Devirleri (İlk Türkçe Dönemi, Eski Türkçe Dönemi)
Hafta 6: Türk Dilinin Gelişmesi ve Tarihi Devirleri (Orta Türkçe, Yeni Türkçe Dönemi)
Hafta 7: Türkçenin Ses Bilgisi (Türkçenin Ses Özellikleri)
Hafta 8: Türkçenin Ses Bilgisi (Türkçede Ses Olayları)
resources: Coşkun, M. V., Türkçenin Ses Bilgisi, İstanbul, 2008.
Türk Dili-I, Eskişehir, 2018. (T.C. Anadolu Üniversitesi Yayını No: 3178; Açıköğretim Fakültesi Yayını No: 2069)
Akar, A., Türk Dili Tarihi, İstanbul, 2005.
Eker, S., Çağdaş Türk Dili, Ankara, 2003
Türk Dili-II, Eskişehir, 2019. (T.C. Anadolu Üniversitesi Yayını No: 2914; Açıköğretim Fakültesi Yayını No: 1871)
Korkmaz, Z.,Ercilasun, A. B., Zülfikar, H., Akalın, M., Gülensoy, T., Parlatır, İ., Binici, N., Yükseköğretim Öğrencileri İçin Türk Dili ve Kompozisyon Bilgileri, Ankara, 1995.

courses.name: Computer Programming
courses.purpose: This course is an introduction to programming. It aims to teach students how to write and read programs in the C programming language, and to gain experience in developing problem-solving steps as algorithms and expressing them in a programming language.
courses.description: An introduction to programming and data structures using the C language.
learning_outcomes: Explain a program written in standard C and how it works
Design software steps (algorithms) for solving a given problem
Translate software steps into the C programming language in a clear and documented way
Produce software components as a member of a software team
resources: Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie, C Programming Language, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Computer Programming Laboratory
courses.purpose: This course is an introduction to programming. It aims to teach students how to write and read programs in the C programming language, and to gain experience in forming problem solutions as algorithmic steps and expressing them in a programming language so they can prepare software that others can use.
courses.description: C programming laboratory applications.
learning_outcomes: Explain a program written in standard C and how it works
Explain a program written in standard C and how it works
Translate software steps into the C programming language in an understandable and documented way
Produce software components as a member of a software team
resources: Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie, C Programming Language, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Culture of Science and Computation
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, öğrencilere bilgisayar mühendisliği ve hesaplama alanının temel kavramlarını, tarihsel gelişimini ve farklı disiplinlerdeki uygulama alanlarını tanıtmaktır. Ayrıca ders, hesaplamanın bilim, mühendislik, ekonomi ve toplum üzerindeki etkilerini ele alarak öğrencilerin bilişim teknolojilerinin rolünü geniş bir mühendislik ve teknoloji kültürü çerçevesinde kavramalarını amaçlamaktadır.
courses.description: Bu derste hesaplama ve bilgisayar mühendisliğinin temel kavramları, bilimsel yaklaşımın temelleri ve bilgisayar sistemlerinin tarihsel gelişimi ele alınmaktadır. Ayrıca büyük veri, nesnelerin interneti, yapay zeka destekli sistemler, biyoteknoloji, finans ve savunma teknolojileri gibi farklı alanlarda hesaplamanın kullanımına ilişkin örnekler incelenerek modern toplumda bilişim ve Ar-Ge kültürü tartışılmaktadır.
learning_outcomes: Öğrenci, hesaplama ve bilgisayar mühendisliğinin temel kavramlarını, tarihsel gelişimini ve bilimsel yaklaşım ile ilişkisini açıklayabilir.
Öğrenci, bilgisayar mühendisliği ve hesaplamanın savunma teknolojileri, biyoteknoloji, finans, büyük veri ve nesnelerin interneti gibi farklı uygulama alanlarındaki rolünü tanımlayabilir.
Öğrenci, modern toplumda bilişim teknolojilerinin ekonomik, sosyal ve bilimsel etkilerini değerlendirebilir ve Ar-Ge kültürünün teknoloji gelişimindeki önemini tartışabilir.
Öğrenci, bilgisayar mühendisliği disiplininin farklı alt alanları hakkında temel farkındalık geliştirerek bu alanların mühendislik problemlerinin çözümündeki kullanımını açıklayabilir.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Bilimsel Yaklaşımın Temelleri, Bilgisayar Sistemlerinin Gelişimi
Hafta 2: Hesaplama ve Bilim İlişkisi ve Hesaplamada Öncü Çalışmalar
Hafta 3: Mühendisliğin tanımı, mühendislik ve hesaplama ilişkisi
Hafta 4: Savunma Teknolojileri alanında Bilim ve Hesaplama
Hafta 7: İş Dünyası, Ekonomi ve Sosyal Bilimlerde Bilim ve Hesaplama
Hafta 10: Biyoteknoloji ve Tıp alanında hesaplama
resources: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/21a-350j-cultures-of-computing-fall-2011/

courses.name: Principles Of Atatürk And The History Of Turkish Revolution II
courses.purpose: Yakın tarih, Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün devrim ve ilkeleri ve Türkiye Cumhuriyeti'nin kazanımları hakkında bilgi vermek.
courses.description: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılâp Tarihi II (AİİT 102) dersi, Millî Mücadele’nin son safhasından başlayarak Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin kuruluş sürecini, inkılapların siyasal, toplumsal, hukuksal, ekonomik ve kültürel boyutlarını ve Cumhuriyet döneminin iç ve dış politika gelişmelerini bütünlüklü biçimde ele alan bir derstir. Ders kapsamında Sevr Antlaşması, Kurtuluş Savaşı cepheleri, Mudanya Mütarekesi ve Lozan Barış Konferansı gibi kritik dönemeçlerin yanı sıra saltanatın kaldırılması, Cumhuriyet’in ilanı, halifeliğin kaldırılması, çok partili hayata geçiş denemeleri, Şeyh Sait İsyanı ve Menemen Olayı gibi erken Cumhuriyet döneminin temel siyasal gelişmeleri incelenmektedir. Ayrıca Atatürk dönemi inkılapları ile bunların toplum, hukuk, eğitim, ekonomi ve kültür alanlarındaki yansımaları, 1923-1939 arası Türk dış politikası, Atatürkçülük ve Atatürk ilkeleri ile İkinci Dünya Savaşı yıllarında Türkiye’nin iç ve dış politikası da dersin ana eksenlerini oluşturmaktadır. Bu yönüyle ders, öğrencilere hem Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin kuruluş felsefesini hem de erken Cumhuriyet döneminin tarihsel dönüşümünü kronolojik ve analitik bir çerçevede kavratmayı amaçlamaktadır.
learning_outcomes: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün öncülüğünde gerçekleşen Türk Devrimi hakkında bilgi sahibi olurlar.
Atatürk İlkeleri ve felsefesini değerlendirir.
Türkiye Cumhuriyeti'nin kazanımlarının tarihi sürecini, bütünüyle tanır.
Geçmiş ve günümüz şartlarını karşılaştırma, bilgi köprüsü kurma yeteneğine sahip olacaklardır.
Günümüz Türkiye’sinin uluslararası sorunları ve kaynakları hakkında bilgi sahibi olacaklar
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Sevr Barış Antlaşması. Kurtuluş Savaşı Cepheler. Güney ve Doğu Cephesi
Hafta 2: Batı Cephesi. Mudanya Mütarekesi; Lozan Barış Konferansı, Lozan Barış Antlaşması’nın Önemli Koşulları-Lozan’ın Getirdikleri

Hafta 3: Saltanatın Kaldırılması, Cumhuriyetin İlan Edilmesi; Birinci TBMM’de Seçim Kararının Alınması; Halk Fırkası’nın (Parti) Kurulması; Ankara’nın Başkent Olması, Cumhuriyetin İlanı ve Tepkiler; Halifeliğin Kaldırılması
Hafta 4: Atatürk Dönemi Çok Partili Hayat Denemeleri, Şeyh Sait İsyanı ve Menemen Olayı
Hafta 5: İnkılaplar ve Hedeflerine Genel Bir Bakış, Ekonomi Alanında Gelişmeler, Eğitim ve Kültür Alanında Gelişmeler
Hafta 6: Toplumsal Alanda Yaşanan Gelişmeler, Hukuk Alanında Yaşanan Gelişmeler
Hafta 8: Ara sınav. Dış politika değerlendirmesi
Hafta 9: İkinci Dünya Savaşı’na Gidiş ve Türk Dış Politikası 1931–1939
Hafta 12: Atatürk Sonrası Türkiye- İsmet İnönü Dönemi
Hafta 13: İkinci Dünya Savaşı Yıllarında İç Politika
Hafta 14: İkinci Dünya Savaşı Yıllarında Dış Politika ve İsmet İnönü Döneminin Sonu
resources: Başlangıcından Günümüze Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Tarihi, (Editör Prof. Dr. Temuçin Faik Ertan),Siyasal Kitabevi, Ankara, 2011

courses.name: Turkish II
courses.purpose: Türk dilinin etkin ve doğru kullanımı ile yazılı ve sözlü anlatım türlerinin kavranması
courses.description: Bu ders, bir yazılı ve sözlü anlatım dersidir. Yazılı anlatım kısmında, öncelikle yöntem ve plan kavramları ve bunların uygulamaları üzerinde durulur. Bilimsel araştırma yazıları ve resmî yazılar ile edebî türler incelenir. Sözlü anlatım kısmındaysa, sözlü iletişimle ilgili genel bilgiler verilir; ardından sözlü edebiyat türleri tartışılır.
learning_outcomes: Türkçeyi yazılı ve sözlü olarak doğru ve akıcı biçimde kullanabilme
Kompozisyon türlerini ayırt edebilmek ve farklı kompozisyon türlerinde yazı yazabilmek
Hazırlayacağı bilimsel yazılara öğrendiği kuralları uyarlayabilmek
Sözlü anlatım türlerini birbirinden ayırabilmek
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Kompozisyonla İlgili Genel Bilgiler Kompozisyon Planı
Hafta 2: Anlatım Teknikleri: Öyküleme, Betimleme, Açıklama, Tartışma
Hafta 3: Düşünceyi Geliştirme Yolları Tanımlama, Karşılaştırma, Örneklendirme, Tanık Gösterme, Kanıtlama, Benzetme
Hafta 7: Yazılı Anlatım Türleri (Öğretici Yazılar): Deneme, Eleştiri, Fıkra (Köşe Yazısı), Seyahatname (Gezi Yazısı), Hatıra, Biyografi, Otobiyografi
Hafta 8: Yazılı Anlatım Türleri (Form Yazılar): Dilekçe, Tutanak, Rapor, Öz geçmiş, Mektup
Hafta 9: Yazılı Anlatım Türleri (Edebî Yazılar): Öykü, Roman, Tiyatro
Hafta 10: Yazılı Anlatım Türleri (Edebî Yazılar): Şiir
Hafta 11: Sözlü Anlatım Türleri (Edebî Anlatılar): Destan, Efsane, Masal, Halk Hikâyesi
Hafta 12: Sözlü Anlatım Türleri: Açık Oturum, Nutuk (Söylev), Konferans, Münazara, Tartışma
Hafta 13: Sözlü Anlatım Türleri: Panel, Forum, Seminer, Kongre, Sempozyum, Açılış Konuşması, Telekonferans
resources: Akar, A., Türk Dili Tarihi, İstanbul, 2005.
Coşkun, M. V., Türkçenin Ses Bilgisi, İstanbul, 2008.
Çotuksöken, Y., Uygulamalı Türk Dili, İstanbul, 2015.
Demir, N., Yılmaz, E., Türk Dili El Kitabı, Ankara, 2005.
Demir, T., Türkçe Dilbilgisi, Ankara, 2004.
Ediskun, H., Türk Dilbilgisi, İstanbul, 2004.
Eker, S., Çağdaş Türk Dili, Ankara, 2003.
Ercilasun, A.B., Başlangıçtan Yirminci Yüzyıla Kadar Türk Dili Tarihi, Amkara, 2004.
Ergin, M., Türk Dil Bilgisi, İstanbul, 2005.
Kolcu, H., Türk Dili, İzmit, 2015.
Macit, M., (ed.), Cavkaytar, S., (ed.), Türk Dili-I, Eskişehir, 2018. (T.C. Anadolu Üniversitesi Yayını No: 3178; Açıköğretim Fakültesi Yayını No: 2069)
Macit, M., (ed.), Cavkaytar, S., (ed.), Türk Dili-II, Eskişehir, 2019. (T.C. Anadolu Üniversitesi Yayını No: 2914; Açıköğretim Fakültesi Yayını No: 1871)
Türk Dili I, Erzurum, 2019. (Atatürk Üniversitesi Açıköğretim Fakültesi Yayını; ISBN: 978-605-7638-28-1)
Türk Dili II, Erzurum, 2013. (Atatürk Üniversitesi Açıköğretim Fakültesi Yayını)
Korkmaz, Z., Ercilasun, A. B., Zülfikar, H., Akalın, M., Gülensoy, T., Parlatır, İ., Binici, N., Yükseköğretim Öğrencileri İçin Türk Dili ve Kompozisyon Bilgileri, Ankara, 1995.

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics
courses.purpose: To provide knowledge about discrete structures and the foundations of discrete mathematics.
courses.description: Propositions, logic, set theory, counting, relations, functions, series, proof methods, recurrence relations, generating functions, groups, finite automata, and graph theory.
learning_outcomes: Define the foundations of discrete structures and discrete mathematics.
Interpret computer applications of discrete mathematics.
Analyze discrete structures.
resources: Discrete Mathematical Structures by Kolman, Busby and Ross
Discrete Mathematics and its applications by Rosen

courses.name: Circuits And Electronics
courses.purpose: The general aim of this course is to introduce electrical and electronic circuit elements that computer engineering students will encounter, and to help them understand the basic operating principles of circuits while gaining the necessary analysis and design skills.
courses.description: This course covers fundamental circuit theorems, the operating principles of passive and active electronic devices (diode, BJT, FET), DC/AC analysis methods, and the realization of logic gates at the transistor level.
learning_outcomes: Analyze relatively simple circuits using various circuit solution methods and circuit theorems.
Calculate the power consumed by passive circuit elements.
Define diode, BJT, FET and OP-AMP structures and DC and AC analysis methods.
Use Ohm's law in the analysis of electrical circuits.
Gain the ability to realize basic logic gates using diode, BJT and MOSFET technology.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: charge, current, voltage and power. Ohm's Law and Kirchhoff's Laws (KCL/KVL).
Hafta 2: Series/parallel circuits, voltage and current dividers. Node and mesh analysis methods.
Hafta 3: Linearity, superposition, source transformation. Thévenin and Norton theorems.
Hafta 4: Inductance (L) and capacitance (C). Solution of first-order (RL and RC) dynamic circuits.
Hafta 5: Sinusoidal analysis and phasors. Average and maximum power. Introduction to Laplace transform.
Hafta 6: Semiconductors and diodes. Diode models and basic application circuits.
Hafta 7: Bipolar Junction Transistors (BJT): structure and operating principles.
Hafta 9: Transistor DC models: biasing and DC analysis methods.
Hafta 11: Small-signal AC models and AC analysis.
Hafta 13: Operational amplifiers (Op-Amps) and application areas.
Hafta 14: Analog-to-Digital Converters (ADC) and Digital-to-Analog Converters (DAC).
resources: Fundamentals of electric circuits, Charles K. Alexander, Matthew N. O. Sadiku. 2000. ISBN 0071160426.
Electronic Devices and Circuit Theory, R. Boylestad and L. Nashelsky, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Circuits And Electronics Laboratory
courses.purpose: This laboratory course is intended to support the topics covered in the circuits and electronics course by providing hands-on practice in a laboratory environment.
courses.description: This laboratory course covers the experimental analysis of the electrical characteristics of electronic circuit elements such as diodes, BJT, MOSFET, and Op-Amp, the design of power amplifiers, and the physical implementation of basic logic gates.
learning_outcomes: Gain general knowledge about diode, BJT, FET and OP-AMP structures and DC and AC analysis methods
Explain methods for modeling time-invariant circuits with discrete circuit elements
Determine the voltage-current relationships of basic circuit elements
Perform measurements with basic laboratory instruments
Analyze and design power amplifiers
Gain the ability to implement basic logic gates using diode, BJT, and MOSFET technology
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is measurement? Electrical measuring instruments
Hafta 3: Ohm's law, mathematical expression of the resistor element. Kirchhoff's current and voltage law experiment
Hafta 4: Voltage and current divider experiment
Hafta 5: Thevenin and Norton theorems experiment
resources: Fundamentals of electric circuits / Charles K. Alexander, Matthew N. O. Sadiku. 2000 ISBN.0071160426

courses.name: Object Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: To introduce the fundamentals of object-oriented programming and the syntax of object-oriented programming.
courses.description: Class, ADT, Inheritance, Dynamic allocation, Design Patterns
learning_outcomes: Program a given problem in C++
Distinguish object-oriented programming from procedural programming
Apply object-oriented programming with standard libraries
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Abstraction and encapsulation in C++
Hafta 11: Comparison of C# and Java with C++
Hafta 13: Comparison of C# and Java with C++
Hafta 14: Comparison of C# and Java with C++
resources: Absolute C++, 5/E Walter Savitch, University of California, San Diego. Kenrick Mock

courses.name: European Union: Institutions and Policy Making
Introduction to Global Warming and Climate Change
Modern Physics for Scientist and Engineers
Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the fundamental data structures and algorithms that can be used in designing software solutions to problems, and to introduce data structures and algorithms.
courses.description: Array, linked list, trees, sorting algorithms, and graphs.
learning_outcomes: List and compare fundamental data structures and algorithms.
Design new algorithms using recognized tools.
Solve complexity analyses of produced solutions.
Understand the hierarchy of data structure implementations in the Java programming language.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Software Design: software life cycle; complexity management; data abstraction; conceptual data structure; program errors; test strategies; program verification.
Hafta 2: Introduction to Algorithm Analysis: computational models; efficiency of algorithms; asymptotic notation; recursive algorithms; experimental analysis.
Hafta 3: Introduction to Algorithm Analysis: continuation.
Hafta 4: Sequential Containers: vector conceptual data structure; vector implementations.
Hafta 5: Sequential Containers: linked list, list conceptual data structure; list and iterator classes; implementations; Standard Template Library.
Hafta 6: Stack: stack class and implementations; applications: palindrome finding; checking correct use of parentheses; evaluating arithmetic expressions.
Hafta 7: Queue: queue class and implementations; applications: simulation of physical systems: waiting queues. Midterm exam.
Hafta 8: Recursion: recursive thinking; manual execution of recursive functions; recursive algorithm design; applications; comparison of recursion and iteration.
Hafta 9: Trees: general structure; tree traversal methods; binary trees; binary search trees; BST class and implementation.
Hafta 10: Trees: heaps; heap implementations; Huffman compression method.
Hafta 11: Map and Set Classes: hash coding; open addressing; chaining; implementations.
Hafta 12: Graphs: definitions; graph class and implementations; graph traversal; topological sorting; graph-based problems: shortest path problems; minimum spanning tree problem.
Hafta 13: Balanced Trees: balanced binary search trees: AVL tree, red-black tree; 2-3 tree; 2-3-4 tree; B-tree.
Hafta 14: Sorting: sorting with STL; selection sort; bubble sort; insertion sort; shell sort; merge sort; heap sort; quick sort; performance comparison.
resources: Elliot Koffman, Paul Wolfgang, Objects, Abstraction, Data Structures and Design Using C++, Wiley.

courses.name: Logic Circuits And Design
courses.purpose: This course provides students with the fundamental knowledge required for the design of hardware-oriented systems. It covers the basic concepts of digital hardware design, including combinational and sequential circuits as well as optimization techniques. The course also introduces design principles that guide the development of digital systems, progressing from simple components to more complex systems, and presents algorithmic state machines for solving complex sequencing problems.
courses.description: Introduction to digital systems, sampling and coding; Boolean algebra and logic gates; combinational logic design and map simplification; decoders, encoders, and multiplexers; arithmetic functions, half adder, full adder, ripple carry adder, and subtractor; sequential circuits and sequential circuit analysis; finite state machines and controllers; registers and register design processes, adders; comparators, counters, multipliers, arithmetic logic unit (ALU), register transfer level (RTL) design, and algorithmic state machines (ASM) and their designs.
learning_outcomes: Explains the components of combinational and sequential circuits
Performs the design, analysis, and implementation of digital circuits using combinational and sequential logic design methods
Decomposes and simplifies circuits
Constructs algorithmic state machine structures to solve digital logic design problems
Uses the fundamental principles and methodologies of computer hardware design
resources: Digital Design, with RTL Design, VHDL, and Verilog, 2nd Edition, by Frank Vahid, John Wiley and Sons Publishers, 2010.

courses.name: Logic Circuits And Design Laboratory
courses.purpose: This course provides laboratory practice and circuit design exercises related to digital hardware systems. It aims to equip students with the fundamental knowledge needed for hardware-oriented system design, covering the basic concepts of digital hardware design including combinational and sequential circuits and optimization techniques. The course also introduces algorithmic state machines for solving complex sequencing problems and provides a basic understanding of how computing systems operate.
courses.description: Introduction to digital systems, sampling and coding; Boolean algebra and logic gates; combinational logic design and map simplifications; decoders, encoders, and multiplexers; arithmetic functions including half adder, full adder, ripple carry adder, and subtractor; sequential circuits and sequential circuit analysis; finite state machines and controllers; registers and register design processes; comparators, counters, multipliers, arithmetic logic unit (ALU); register transfer level (RTL) design; algorithmic state machines (ASM) and their designs.
learning_outcomes: Explains the components of combinational and sequential circuits.
Uses combinational and sequential logic design methods to design, analyze, and implement digital circuits.
Decomposes and simplifies circuits.
Constructs algorithmic state machine structures to solve digital logic design problems.
Uses the basic principles and methodologies of computer hardware design.
resources: Digital Design, with RTL Design, VHDL, and Verilog, 2nd Edition, by Frank Vahid, John Wiley and Sons Publishers, 2010.

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: To build a strong foundation in linear algebra concepts and enable students to apply these concepts to engineering problems, including data analysis, graphics, and machine learning.
courses.description: Vectors, matrices and their operations, systems of linear equations, determinants, vector spaces, subspaces, basis and dimension, linear transformations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, diagonalization, inner product spaces, orthogonality, least squares problems, SVD and other basic algorithms, and applications in engineering fields.
learning_outcomes: Solve systems of linear equations using matrix methods
Analyze vector spaces and linear transformations
Compute and interpret eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Apply linear algebra techniques to engineering problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Vectors, matrices and their operations
Hafta 9: Linear transformations and their applications
Hafta 11: Markov chains and the PageRank algorithm
Hafta 13: Least squares problem and regression
Hafta 14: SVD and TSVD algorithms and applications
resources: Linear Algebra and Its Applications - Gilbert Strang
Linear Algebra Done Right - Sheldon Axler

courses.name: Introduction to Algorithm Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students fundamental algorithms that can be used to solve problems encountered in daily life, and to analyze and compare their performance from various aspects such as running time, memory usage, and energy consumption, as well as to introduce algorithm design methods.
courses.description: The course covers algorithm basics, algorithm analysis, recursive functions and solution methods, direct algorithm design by brute force, divide-and-conquer, decrease-and-conquer, transform-and-conquer, dynamic programming, greedy programming, incremental development, and complexity classes including P, NP, and NP-complete problems.
learning_outcomes: Design solutions for newly encountered problems using algorithm design methods.
Analyze an algorithm according to criteria such as running time and memory usage.
Distinguish between algorithm design methods.
Select the most appropriate alternative algorithm based on performance criteria.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Background: discrete mathematics; data structures. Introduction to algorithms: what is an algorithm; various problems. Algorithm analysis: algorithm complexity; asymptotic notation.
Hafta 2: Recursive functions and solution methods: substitution method; characteristic equation method; master theorem.
Hafta 3: Direct algorithm design by brute force: sorting algorithms; searching algorithms; sequence matching problem; closest pair problem; convex hull problem; exhaustive search method.
Hafta 4: Divide-and-conquer method: sorting algorithms; searching algorithms; Strassen matrix multiplication algorithm; closest pair problem; convex hull problem; integer multiplication problem.

Hafta 5: Decrease-and-conquer method: sorting algorithms; graph traversal algorithms, depth-first and breadth-first; topological sorting; algorithms for generating combinatorial objects; counterfeit coin problem; selection problem.
Hafta 6: Transform-and-conquer method: sorting by sorting; Gaussian elimination algorithm; balanced search trees; heaps and heap sort; Horner's rule and binary exponentiation; problem reduction.
Hafta 8: Dynamic programming method: 0/1 knapsack problem; all-pairs shortest paths; optimal binary search tree; sequence matching; matrix chain multiplication.
Hafta 10: Greedy programming method: knapsack problem; minimum spanning tree; single-source shortest paths; deadline scheduling; Huffman tree; activity selection problem.
Hafta 12: Incremental development method; simplex method; maximum flow problem.
Hafta 13: Complexity classes; basic definitions; P, NP and NP-complete classes; NP-complete problems.
resources: Jon Kleinberg, Eva Tardos, "Algorithm Design", Pearson.
Anany Levitin, "Introduction To The Design And Analysis Of Algorithms", Addison Wesley.

courses.name: Computer Organization
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with knowledge about digital integrated circuit (IC) technology used in the production of digital circuits and to introduce the components of integrated circuits. It also informs students about integrated circuit design, power and timing analysis, simulation and optimization, and manufacturing stages, as well as design tools such as SPICE and layout tools such as Magic.
courses.description: This course covers digital integrated circuit (IC) manufacturing technologies, circuit components, and the design, simulation, power analysis, and timing analysis processes performed using SPICE and layout tools (such as Magic).
learning_outcomes: Understands the digital integrated circuit (IC) technology used in the production of digital circuits
Can solve the problems of integrated circuit design, power and timing analysis, simulation and optimization using theoretical methods
Can solve power, speed, and connectivity problems by combining theory and practice using computer-aided digital integrated circuit design and analysis tools
Distinguishes the components of integrated circuits
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The importance and difficulty of digital integrated circuits
Hafta 2: Structure and function of the transistor
Hafta 4: CMOS fabrication and layout rules
Hafta 5: Pass transistors, transmission gates, and the Magic layout tool
Hafta 6: MIPS processor design stages and Spice
Hafta 10: Elmore delay model for best- and worst-case delay calculation
Hafta 14: General review and final project
resources: N. Weste, D. Harris, Integrated Circuit Design, 4th Edition, Pearson, 2011

courses.name: Programming Languages
courses.purpose: This course provides students with important knowledge about programming languages, example programming languages, syntactic and semantic analysis, names and scope, data types, arrays, arithmetic operations, control structures, subprograms, abstraction and encapsulation, object-oriented programming language categories, concurrency, exception handling, and functional programming languages.
courses.description: This course provides students with important knowledge about programming languages, example programming languages, syntactic and semantic analysis, names and scope, data types, arrays, arithmetic operations, control structures, subprograms, abstraction and encapsulation, object-oriented programming language categories, concurrency, exception handling, and functional programming languages.
learning_outcomes: List the historical development of programming languages.
Choose the right language for a given task.
Distinguish different types of languages from each other.
resources: Robert W. Sebesta, Concepts of Programming Languages, 11th ed., Addison-Wesley.

courses.name: Software Engineering
courses.purpose: This course introduces the theory and practice of software engineering. It covers current methods and techniques used in different stages of the software development life cycle, such as requirements analysis, design, implementation, testing, and maintenance. The aim of the course is to enable students to apply software engineering techniques to a real project within a project team using the latest technologies.
courses.description: Introduction to Software Engineering; Software Development Life Cycle Models; Agile Software Development; Requirements Engineering; Project Management and Planning; System Modeling; Design Principles; Software Testing and Evolution; Systems Engineering.
learning_outcomes: Understand software engineering principles, methods, and practices.
Define and compare different software development life cycle models (e.g., Waterfall, Agile, Spiral).
Apply appropriate life cycle models to different software projects.
Elicit, analyze, and document software requirements effectively.
Use modeling techniques (e.g., UML diagrams) to represent software systems.
Apply project management concepts such as planning, scheduling, and risk assessment.
Work effectively in a team using version control systems and collaboration tools such as Git.
Develop and deploy a real software project within a team.
resources: Sommerville I., Software Engineering, 10th ed. (2016), Prentice Hall
Software Engineering, 10th Edition, Ian Sommerville, Pearson.
The Devops Handbook: How to Create World-Class Agility, Reliability, and Security in Technology Organizations, Gene Kim, Patrick Debois and John Willis, Trade Select.

courses.name: Summer Practice I
courses.purpose: To guide students in deciding on their career goals and building a professional foundation, while providing opportunities to strengthen and apply their theoretical knowledge.
courses.description: This course aims to guide students in identifying their career goals in the field of engineering, reinforce their theoretical knowledge through practice, and support them in building a professional foundation. Students work in an institution or industrial enterprise for the specified period, gaining the opportunity to observe and directly experience engineering practices. Activities during the internship include corporate operations, engineering processes, ethical practices, and teamwork.
learning_outcomes: Recognize and identify the applications of theoretical knowledge acquired during university education
Gain professional experience
Observe the operation of a company
Present professional observations and experiences in an official report
Use profession-specific technical equipment
resources: Books, manuals, plans, and all kinds of materials used at the internship institution.

courses.name: Differential Equations
courses.purpose: To teach ordinary differential equations used in engineering systems, their applications, and fundamental solution methods.
courses.description: Basic definitions, separable equations and equations reducible to this form, linear and nonlinear equations, exact differential equations and integrating factors, special integrating factors, applications to mechanics and physics, rate problems, initial value problems and formulation, basic existence and uniqueness theorems, theory and solutions of higher-order linear differential equations, constant-coefficient homogeneous linear differential equations, method of undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters, Cauchy-Euler equations, variable-coefficient linear equations, reduction of order, series solutions, power series solutions near a point, power series around regular singular points and the Frobenius method, Laplace transform, existence and basic properties, inverse Laplace transform, convolution, solutions of constant-coefficient linear differential equations by Laplace transform, systems of homogeneous linear differential equations, solution techniques for nonhomogeneous systems, and brief applications of nonlinear differential equations.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic solution methods of differential equations.
Gain mathematical application skills to other sciences and engineering.
Develop mathematical modeling ability.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, course content, objectives and grading. Basic definitions, directional fields.
Hafta 2: First-order differential equations: linear differential equations, separable equations, exact equations, Bernoulli differential equations.
Hafta 3: First-order differential equations: modeling, equilibrium solutions, applications of first-order equations.
Hafta 4: Second-order differential equations: basic concepts, real and distinct roots, complex roots, repeated roots, reduction of order.
Hafta 5: Second-order differential equations: fundamental solution sets, Wronskian, nonhomogeneous differential equations, method of undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters.
Hafta 6: Numerical solutions: Euler method, higher-order Taylor methods, Runge-Kutta methods, numerical applications.
Hafta 7: Review of topics, problem solving, and midterm exam.
Hafta 8: Series solutions: introduction to power series, power series method, singular points, Frobenius method, Legendre polynomials.
Hafta 9: Laplace transforms: basic concepts, properties and examples of Laplace transforms, solving differential equations with Laplace transforms.
Hafta 10: Transforms: step and impulse functions, convolution theorem, systems of differential equations.
Hafta 11: Linear systems, applications, matrix formulations.
Hafta 12: Linear systems: eigenvalue problem, solution of constant-coefficient systems in 2D, theory of homogeneous constant-coefficient systems, nonhomogeneous systems.
Hafta 14: Partial differential equations: wave equation, heat equation, vibrating string.
resources: An Introduction to Ordinary Differential Equations (Earl A. Coddington)
Ordinary Differential Equations (I. G. Petrovski)
Russell Herman, “A First Course in Differential Eqautions for Scientists and Engineers”, LibreTexts, 2023.

courses.name: The Art of Adaptation: Storytelling Across Media
Operating Systems
courses.purpose: To evaluate the fundamental operating principles of operating systems. It aims to provide students with general principles about the structure of operating systems and equip them with the basic knowledge and skills needed to design an operating system.
courses.description: This course covers operating system architectures, the working principles of core components such as process and memory management, the analysis of performance problems including deadlock and synchronization, and the design processes of a modern operating system.
learning_outcomes: Evaluate how the basic components of an operating system work.
Analyze the structure of well-known and widely used operating systems and their fundamental differences.
Gain the ability to identify performance-related problems in operating systems and propose solutions to these problems.
Design a modern operating system.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Process concept. Batch processes. Historical development of the process concept. Principle of running multiple processes on a single processor at the same time.
Hafta 3: Process scheduling and states methods. FCFS, SJF, RR methods.
Hafta 4: Thread approach and its advantages and disadvantages.
Hafta 5: Classical synchronization problems. Producer-Consumer problem. Semaphores.
Hafta 6: Dining Philosophers problem, the Banker algorithm, and advanced methods and problem analysis in synchronization of different processes
Hafta 7: Deadlock situations and deadlock diagrams. Algorithms for deadlock detection and prevention.
Hafta 9: Memory management. Address relocation and MMU approaches.
Hafta 10: Virtual memory approach. Paging and page fault situations. Thrashing.
Hafta 11: Disk management. Disk management algorithms. RAID.
Hafta 12: File system and file systems.
resources: Modern Operating Systems, 5th edition Andrew S. Tanenbaum

courses.name: Numerical Analysis in Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' ability to design, analyze, and implement numerical algorithms under real-world constraints such as finite precision, computational cost, and large-scale data. It also aims to enable the use of these skills in areas such as machine learning, graphs, networks, and scientific computing.
courses.description: Floating-point arithmetic, error analysis, linear and nonlinear equations, numerical linear algebra, optimization, interpolation, numerical differentiation, eigenvalue computation, and dimensionality reduction.
learning_outcomes: Analyze numerical algorithms in terms of stability and complexity.
Code and compare numerical methods.
Apply numerical methods to data-driven problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Floating-point arithmetic, rounding, and machine precision.
Hafta 2: Error propagation, conditioning, and stability.
Hafta 3: Treating root finding as search algorithms: bisection method and fixed-point iteration.
Hafta 4: Treating root finding as search algorithms: Newton and Secant methods.
Hafta 5: Linear systems: Gaussian elimination and LU decomposition.
Hafta 6: Iterative solvers: Jacobi and Gauss-Seidel methods.
Hafta 7: Data fitting and approximation methods: interpolation and splines.
Hafta 12: Google PageRank algorithm and Markov chains.
Hafta 13: Numerical optimization methods: gradient descent.
resources: Numerical Linear Algebra by Lloyd N. Trefethen & David Bau
Matrix Computations by Gene H. Golub & Charles H. Van Loan

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach engineering students the basic statistical concepts at a level where they can use them effectively.
courses.description: Probability, random variables, distributions, and statistical inference.
learning_outcomes: Applies basic statistical concepts to engineering problems encountered.
Obtains probability distribution functions of random variables based on real-life scientific scenarios and data sets, and uses them to find expected value and variance.
Explains the fundamental concepts of probability theory.
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: Continuous and discrete random variables
Hafta 7: Probability distribution functions of random variables
Hafta 8: Probability density functions of random variables
Hafta 9: Midterm exam, Gaussian, binomial distributions
Hafta 13: Expected values of random variables
resources: Probability of Statistics for Engineering & Scientists. Walpole E.W., Myers R.H., Myers S.L., Ye K. Pearson Education, Prentice Hall Inc.

courses.name: Numerical Analysis
courses.purpose: To develop the ability to design, analyze, and implement numerical algorithms under real-world constraints such as finite precision, computational cost, and large-scale data, with applications in machine learning, graphics, networks, and scientific computing.
courses.description: Floating-point arithmetic, error analysis, linear and nonlinear equations, numerical linear algebra, optimization, interpolation, numerical differentiation, eigenvalue computation, and dimensionality reduction.

courses.name: Standardization and Quality Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide engineering students who will enter professional life with fundamental knowledge in standardization, metrology, and quality management. It also aims to develop awareness of total quality management, understanding of quality assurance, and the ability to relate these concepts to national and international standards so that students can use and interpret standards in production, design, and R&D processes. In addition, the course seeks to equip students with the ability to prepare and present project proposals as a multidisciplinary team.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts and applications of standardization and quality management systems. It introduces the definition, purpose, and principles of standardization, the benefits for producers and consumers, standardization organizations in Turkey and worldwide, and the processes of preparing standards. It also provides general information on metrology, measurement uncertainty, calibration, CE marking, and related regulations. The course further addresses quality concepts, factors affecting quality, quality management systems, internal control processes, risk analysis and management, and management system standards such as ISO 9001, ISO 50001, and ISO 45001. In addition, advanced topics related to quality assurance, including accreditation, method validation, and conformity assessment, are covered.
learning_outcomes: Gain knowledge and skills about standardization and quality management.
Become knowledgeable about CE markings and harmonized standards.
Acquire the knowledge and skills to operate a quality management system.
Have basic knowledge to perform measurement uncertainty calculations.
Be able to read and understand calibration certificates.
Have the knowledge to handle conformity assessment processes in a holistic manner.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course introduction and basic concepts related to standardization
Hafta 2: Purposes and principles of standardization, benefits for producers and consumers
Hafta 3: Standard preparation processes and standardization organizations in the world and in Turkey
Hafta 4: General information about metrology and measurement uncertainty
Hafta 5: General information about calibration reporting and planning
Hafta 6: General information about CE marking and regulations
Hafta 7: Quality concept, quality management systems
Hafta 8: Midterm exam, internal control processes, factors affecting quality
Hafta 10: Management system standards (ISO 9001 quality) and applications
Hafta 11: Management system standards (ISO 50001 energy) and applications
Hafta 12: Management system standards (ISO 45001 occupational health and safety) and applications
resources: TS ISO/IEC Guide 99 (2013), International vocabulary of metrology - Basic and general concepts and associated terms (VIM).
BIPM (2008). JCGM 100:2008 Evaluation of measurement data — Guide to the expression of uncertainty in measurement.
TS ISO/IEC 17025, General requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories.
